content:
Ch 17
This chapter should help students sort out questions of goals, tone, format, and style that help them understand the expectations of college writing that they face in your class and, especially, in other classes that do not make their writing expectations quite as explicit.

Even though the standards for academic argument are high, students shouldn’t be intimidated by the prospect of writing for academic audiences. They do not have to become the world’s expert on a particular topic to display an appropriate and impressive level of expertise: encourage them to use this chapter to familiarize them-selves with the conventions of academic argument. Understanding the features of academic argument can prepare them for successful writing across the curriculum and help change their minds about some of the myths of academic writing.
